description_long: This object consists of a cluster of large, irregularly shaped dark stone blocks that appear to have tumbled onto a section of railway track. The stones are jagged and rough, with angular surfaces and a matte finish, suggesting they are made of natural rock. The railway track, partially buried beneath the stones, features wooden ties and metal rails, and a small brown mining cart is wedged among the rocks. The stones dominate the scene, blocking the track completely and creating a formidable barrier. The overall appearance suggests a landslide or rockfall scenario, with the stones acting as an impassable obstacle. There are no visible engravings or markings on the stones, and the surface shows no significant weathering.
description: A cluster of large, jagged dark stone blocks has fallen across a railway track, completely blocking it. A small mining cart is wedged among the rocks, emphasizing the obstruction.
description_view_0: From this view, the railway track and mining cart are clearly visible, with the stone blocks piled up and blocking the track.
description_view_1: This view shows the stone blocks head-on, obscuring the track and cart almost entirely behind the mass of rock.
description_view_2: The side view highlights the depth and irregularity of the stone pile, with the mining cart partially visible among the rocks.
description_view_3: Looking straight down the track, the stones are seen completely blocking the railway, with the mining cart directly in front of the obstruction.
attribute_location_context: urban railway or industrial trackway obstructed by rockfall
attribute_installation_method: naturally fallen or deposited, not intentionally installed
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear freshly broken and angular.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: blocks and covers a section of railway track, partially integrating with the rails and cart
